question_text: Which statement best describes the law of conservation of energy?

options.label: A
options.text: Energy can be created when we burn fuel

options.label: B
options.text: Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only transferred or transformed

options.label: C
options.text: Energy always stays in the same form

options.label: D
options.text: Energy disappears when machines stop working

explanation: The law of conservation of energy states that energy cannot be created or destroyed, but it can be transferred from one place to another or transformed from one form to another. This is a fundamental law of physics.
